plugins.name: nl-asana-ticket
plugins.description: Standardized Asana ticket creation with project routing, custom fields, and formatting for Crescendo Lab

plugins.name: nl-bq-analysis
plugins.description: BigQuery analysis skill with mandatory dry-run cost estimation, reusable query patterns, and cost-aware execution for any GCP project

plugins.name: nl-code-review
plugins.description: General-purpose code review skill that auto-detects tech stack and applies repo-aware review with optional adversarial pass

plugins.name: nl-handle-issue
plugins.description: Single entry point for issue resolution: classifies scope, orchestrates investigation with mandatory root-cause report, and chains sub-skills from debugging to PR creation

plugins.name: nl-living-docs
plugins.description: Detect documentation drift from code changes with severity-ranked reports and actionable diff suggestions

plugins.name: nl-pr-comment-resolve
plugins.description: Resolve PR review comments with structured workflow: fetch, evaluate, implement, commit, and reply with commit SHA links

plugins.name: nl-research-plan-execute
plugins.description: Structured feature development workflow: codebase research, annotated planning with human review cycles, and subagent-driven execution with dual review gates

plugins.name: nl-resource-digest
plugins.description: Deep analysis of public resources (URLs, articles, GitHub repos, tweets, tech news) with critical opinions, pros/cons, and actionable insights

plugins.name: nl-terminal-diagrams
plugins.description: Render precise, perfectly-aligned diagrams in the terminal using graph-easy. Covers flowcharts, architecture diagrams, and relationship graphs with Unicode boxart output

plugins.name: nl-create-pr
plugins.description: Create pull requests with FoD scope check gate, develop-first branching, conventional commits, and structured PR body template

plugins.name: nl-stack-pr
plugins.description: Manage stacked pull request chains: visualize, add, update, and collapse PRs as upstream branches merge using rebase --onto

plugins.name: nl-learn
plugins.description: Structured learning companion for articles, PDFs, and transcripts — with quick/deep modes, concept maps, self-test questions, and next steps

plugins.name: nl-kb
plugins.description: Personal knowledge base workflow: collect raw resources and compile into structured wiki

plugins.name: nl-playlist-curation
plugins.description: Curate Spotify playlists with O(?) complexity notation naming and point-of-view track selection (15-20 tracks per playlist)

plugins.name: nl-writing-skills
plugins.description: Write and edit Claude Code skills with a TDD-driven process (baseline-test, write, refactor) fused with a design vocabulary (predictability, leading words, information hierarchy) for building predictable skills

plugins.name: nl-grill
plugins.description: Relentless one-at-a-time interrogation that turns a rough idea into an approved, written spec — a grilling engine fused with a brainstorming pipeline, with an explicit problem-vs-solution split

plugins.name: nl-domain-modeling
plugins.description: Build a durable ubiquitous-language glossary and record hard-to-reverse decisions as ADRs — captures the domain model in files as the single source of truth, not the chat transcript

plugins.name: nl-handoff
plugins.description: Compact the current session into a durable, structured, secret-redacted handoff doc a fresh agent can resume from cold — saved to the repo's plan tree with a concrete entry point

plugins.name: nl-index
plugins.description: Router and map for the nl-* skill collection — which skill to reach for in a given situation, how they chain, and where the collisions and gaps are

plugins.name: nl-debug
plugins.description: Loop-first discipline for hard bugs — build a tight red-capable reproduction loop before hypothesising, minimise it into a regression test; extends superpowers systematic-debugging with Matt Pocock's diagnosing-bugs feedback-loop rigor

plugins.name: nl-plan-to-tickets
plugins.description: Slice an agreed feature plan into independently-shippable vertical-slice (tracer-bullet) tickets and publish them to the tracker in dependency order — not horizontal layer tickets
